codeReview.summary: The implementation fulfills all three validation criteria (VAL-MAP-005, VAL-MAP-006, VAL-MAP-007). Hover tooltip shows department code and name, clicking dept 49 navigates using Inertia router, and clicking other departments shows a 'coming soon' tooltip. The code passes type checking and linting. However, there are some UX concerns with the 'coming soon' tooltip implementation.

issues.description: The 'coming soon' tooltip uses `fixed inset-0 z-50` which creates a full-screen overlay blocking all user interaction for 2 seconds. A less intrusive toast notification near the map or cursor would provide better UX.

The 'coming soon' tooltip does not indicate WHICH department was clicked. User clicks dept 44, sees generic 'Bientôt disponible' message but cannot confirm which department triggered it. Consider showing the department name/code in the tooltip.
The tooltip auto-dismisses after 2 seconds with no user-controllable dismiss option. Users should be able to click/tap to dismiss early if desired.
The tooltip uses hardcoded French text 'Bientôt disponible !' and 'Ce département sera bientôt accessible'. Consider making these strings internationalizable (i18n) for future multi-language support.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation follows the existing patterns in the codebase: Inertia router usage in DepartementsIndex, state management via useState, component prop interface pattern. No convention violations detected.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: router.visit() is used correctly in Departements/Index.tsx following Inertia.js patterns; FranceMap props interface follows existing component patterns

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the frontend-worker skill procedure correctly. All required checks (npm run types, npm run lint) passed. No deviations noted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true with no deviations

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly identified that PHP/Laravel backend was not available in the environment for full-stack testing. The validation contract mentions using agent-browser for manual verification but the worker noted that full browser testing couldn't be completed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 'whatWasLeftUndone' notes: 'Full end-to-end browser testing could not be performed because Laravel backend (PHP) is not available in this environment.'

summary: Implementation passes validation criteria. The feature correctly implements: hover tooltip (VAL-MAP-005), navigation for dept 49 using Inertia router (VAL-MAP-006), and 'coming soon' tooltip for non-49 departments (VAL-MAP-007). Code quality is good with proper TypeScript types, correct Inertia usage for SPA navigation, and clean component structure. Minor UX improvements suggested for the 'coming soon' tooltip (show which department, less intrusive placement, dismissible).
